params.description: Internally used identification parameter for an OSPF routing process. It is locally assigned, and can be a positive integer. A unique value is assigned for each OSPF routing process.

params.name: vrf vrf-name
params.description: (Optional) Specifies the name of the VPN routing and forwarding (VRF) instance to associate with the OSPF VRF processes.

usageSummary: You can specify multiple OSPF routing processes in each router.  After you enter the router ospf command, you can enter the maximum number of paths.  There can be between 1 and 32 paths.

name: router ospfv3
detail: Enter Open Shortest Path First Version 3 (OSPFv3) through router configuration mode.

params.name: process-id
params.description: (Optional) Internal identification. The number that is used here is the number assigned administratively when enabling the OSPFv3 routing process. The range is 1-65535.

usageSummary: Use the router ospfv3 command to enter OSPFv3 router configuration mode.  From this mode, you can enter taddress-family configuration mode for IPv6 or IPv4, and then configure the IPv6 or IPv4 address family.

name: send-lifetime
detail: Set the time period during which an authentication key on a key chain is valid to be sent.
syntax: send-lifetime [ local ] start-time { infinite end-time | duration seconds }

params.name: local
params.description: Specifies the time in local timezone.

params.name: start-time
params.description: Beginning time that the key specified by the key command is valid to be sent. The syntax can be either of the following: hh : mm : ss month date year hh : mm : ss date month year • hh: Hours • mm: Minutes • ss: Seconds • month: First three letters of the month • date: Date (1-31) • year: Year (four digits) The default start time and the earliest acceptable date is January 1, 1993.

params.name: infinite
params.description: Key is valid to be sent from the start-time value on.

params.name: end-time
params.description: Key is valid to be sent from the start-time value until the end-timevalue. The syntax is the same as that for the start-timevalue.The end-time value must be after the start-timevalue. The default end time is an infinite time period.

params.name: duration seconds
params.description: Length of time (in seconds) that the key is valid to be sent. The range is from 1 to 2147483646.

usageSummary: Specify a start-time value and one of the following values: infinite, end-time, or duration seconds.  Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17. 15.

name: set community
detail: Set the BGP communities attribute.

params.name: community-number
params.description: Specifies that community number. Valid values are from 1 to 4294967200, no-export, or no-advertise.

params.name: additive
params.description: (Optional) Adds the community to the already existing communities.

params.name: well-known-community
params.description: (Optional) Well know communities can be specified by using the following keywords: • internet • local-as • no-advertise • no-export

params.name: none
params.description: (Optional) Removes the community attribute from the prefixes that pass the route map.

usageSummary: You must have a match clause (even if it points to a “permit everything” list) if you want to set tags.  Use the route-map global configuration command, and the match and set route map configuration commands, to define the conditions for redistributing routes from one routing protocol into another.  Each route-map command has a list of match and set commands associated with it.

name: set ip next-hop
detail: Indicate where to output packets that pass a match clause of a route map for policy routing.

params.name: ip-address
params.description: IP address of the next hop to which packets are output. It need not be an adjacent router.

params.name: peer-address
params.description: (Optional) Sets the next hop to be the BGP peering address.

usageSummary: An ellipsis (... ) in the command syntax indicates that your command input can include multiple values for the ip-address argument.  Use the ip policy route-map interface configuration command, the route-map global configuration command, and the match and set route-map configuration commands to define the conditions for policy routing packets.

name: show ip bgp
detail: Display entries in the Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) routing table.
syntax: show ip bgp [ip-address [mask [longer-prefixes [injected] | shorter-prefixes [length] | best-path-reason | bestpath | multipaths | subnets] | best-path-reason | bestpath | internal | multipaths] | all | oer-paths | prefix-list name | pending-prefixes | route-map name | version {version-number | recent offset-value}]

params.name: ip-address
params.description: (Optional) IP address entered to filter the output to display only a particular host or network in the BGP routing table.

params.name: mask
params.description: (Optional) Mask to filter or match hosts that are part of the specified network.

params.name: longer-prefixes
params.description: (Optional) Displays the specified route and all more-specific routes.

params.name: injected
params.description: (Optional) Displays more-specific prefixes injected into the BGP routing table.

params.name: shorter-prefixes
params.description: (Optional) Displays the specified route and all less-specific routes.

params.name: length
params.description: (Optional) The prefix length. The range is a number from 0 to 32.

params.name: bestpath
params.description: (Optional) Displays the best path for this prefix.

params.name: best-path-reason
params.description: (Optional) Displays the reason why a path loses to the bestpath. Note If the best-path is yet to be selected, then the output will be 'Best Path Evaluation: No best path'

params.name: internal
params.description: (Optional) Displays the internal details for this prefix.

params.name: multipaths
params.description: (Optional) Displays multipaths for this prefix.

params.name: subnets
params.description: (Optional) Displays the subnet routes for the specified prefix.

params.name: all
params.description: (Optional) Displays all address family information in the BGP routing table.

params.name: oer-paths
params.description: (Optional) Displays Optimized Edge Routing (OER) controlled prefixes in the BGP routing table.

params.name: prefix-list name
params.description: (Optional) Filters the output based on the specified prefix list.

params.name: pending-prefixes
params.description: (Optional) Displays prefixes that are pending deletion from the BGP routing table.

params.name: route-map name
params.description: (Optional) Filters the output based on the specified route map. version version-number (Optional) Displays all prefixes with network versions greater than or equal to the specified version number. The range is from 1 to 4294967295.

params.name: recent offset-value
params.description: (Optional) Displays the offset from the current routing table version. The range is from 1 to 4294967295. Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.15.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 1637 Routing show ip bgp

usageSummary: The show ip bgp command is used to display the contents of the BGP routing table.  The output can be filtered to display entries for a specific prefix, prefix length, and prefixes injected through a prefix list, route map, or conditional advertisement.  When changes are made to the network address, the network version number is incremented.

name: show ip bgp neighbors
detail: Display information about Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) and TCP connections to neighbors.
syntax: show ip bgp [ipv4 {multicast | unicast} | vpnv4 all | vpnv6 unicast all] neighbors [slowip-address | ipv6-address [advertised-routes | dampened-routes | flap-statistics | paths [reg-exp] | policy [detail] | received prefix-filter | received-routes | routes]]

params.name: ipv4
params.description: (Optional) Displays peers in the IPv4 address family.

params.name: multicast
params.description: (Optional) Specifies IPv4 multicast address prefixes.

params.name: unicast
params.description: (Optional) Specifies IPv4 unicast address prefixes.

params.name: vpnv4 all
params.description: (Optional) Displays peers in the VPNv4 address family.

params.name: vpnv6 unicast all
params.description: (Optional) Displays peers in the VPNv6 address family.

params.name: slow
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about dynamically configured slow peers.

params.name: ip-address
params.description: (Optional) IP address of the IPv4 neighbor. If this argument is omitted, information about all neighbors is displayed.

params.name: ipv6-address
params.description: (Optional) IP address of the IPv6 neighbor.

params.name: advertised-routes
params.description: (Optional) Displays all routes that have been advertised to neighbors.

params.name: dampened-routes
params.description: (Optional) Displays the dampened routes received from the specified neighbor.

params.name: flap-statistics
params.description: (Optional) Displays the flap statistics of the routes learned from the specified neighbor (for external BGP peers only).

params.name: paths reg-exp
params.description: (Optional) Displays autonomous system paths learned from the specified neighbor. An optional regular expression can be used to filter the output.

params.name: policy
params.description: (Optional) Displays the policies applied to this neighbor per address family.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed policy information such as route maps, prefix lists, community lists, access control lists (ACLs), and autonomous system path filter lists.

params.name: received prefix-filter
params.description: (Optional) Displays the prefix list (outbound route filter [ORF]) sent from the specified neighbor.

params.name: received-routes
params.description: (Optional) Displays all received routes (both accepted and rejected) from the specified neighbor.

params.name: routes
params.description: (Optional) Displays all routes that are received and accepted. The output displayed when this keyword is entered is a subset of the output displayed by the received-routes keyword. Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.15.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 1649 Routing show ip bgp neighbors

usageSummary: Use the show ip bgp neighbors command to display BGP and TCP connection information for neighbor sessions.  For BGP, this includes detailed neighbor attribute, capability, path, and prefix information.  For TCP, this includes statistics related to BGP neighbor session establishment and maintenance.

name: show ip bgp ipv6 unicast
detail: Display entries in the Internet Protocol version 6 (IPv6) Border Gateway Protocol (BGP) routing table.
syntax: show ip bgp ipv6 unicast [ prefix / length ]
usageSummary: The show ip bgp ipv6 unicast command provides output similar to the show ip bgp command, except that it is IPv6 specific.

name: show ip eigrp accounting
detail: Display prefix accounting information for Enhanced Interior Gateway Routing Protocol (EIGRP) processes.
syntax: show ip eigrp accounting [ vrf { vrf-name | * }] [ autonomous-system-number ] accounting

params.name: vrfvrf-name
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the specified VRF.

params.name: vrf *
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about all VRFs. autonomous-system-number (Optional) Autonomous system number.

usageSummary: This command can be used to display information about EIGRP named configurations and EIGRP autonomous-system (AS) configurations.

name: show ip eigrp interfaces
detail: Display information about interfaces that are configured for the Enhanced Interior Gateway Routing Protocol (EIGRP).
syntax: show ip eigrp [vrf vrf-name] [autonomous-system-number] interfaces [type number] [detail]

params.name: vrf vrf-name
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the specified virtual routing and forwarding (VRF) instance. autonomous-system-number (Optional) Autonomous system number whose output needs to be filtered.

params.name: type
params.description: (Optional) Interface type. For more information, use the question mark (?) online help function.

params.name: number
params.description: (Optional) Interface or subinterface number. For more information about the numbering syntax for your networking device, use the question mark (?) online help function.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed information about EIGRP interfaces for a specific EIGRP process.

usageSummary: Use the show ip eigrp interfaces command to display active EIGRP interfaces and EIGRP-specific interface settings and statistics.  The optional type number argument and the detail keyword can be entered in any order.  If an interface is specified, only information about that interface is displayed.

name: show ip eigrp neighbors
detail: Display neighbors discovered by the Enhanced Interior Gateway Routing Protocol (EIGRP).
syntax: show ip eigrp [vrf vrf-name] [autonomous-system-number] neighbors [static | detail] [interface-type interface-number]

params.name: vrf vrf-name
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the specified VPN Routing and Forwarding (VRF) instance.

params.name: autonomous-system-number
params.description: (Optional) Autonomous-system-number-specific output is displayed.

params.name: static
params.description: (Optional) Displays static neighbors.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays detailed neighbor information. interface-type interface-number (Optional) Interface-specific output is displayed.

usageSummary: The show ip eigrp neighbors command can be used to display information about EIGRP named and EIGRP autonomous-system configurations.  Use the show ip eigrp neighbors command to display dynamic and static neighbor states.  You can use this command for also debugging certain types of transport problems.

name: show ip eigrp topology
detail: Display Enhanced Interior Gateway Routing Protocol (EIGRP) topology table entries.
syntax: show ip eigrp topology [ network [ mask ] prefix | active | all-links | detail-links | pending | secondary-paths | summary | zero-successors ]

params.name: network
params.description: (Optional) Network address.

params.name: mask
params.description: (Optional) Network mask.

params.name: prefix
params.description: (Optional) Network prefix in the format <network>/<length>, for example, 192.168.0.0/16.

params.name: active
params.description: (Optional) Displays all topology entries that are in the active state.

params.name: all-links
params.description: (Optional) Displays all the entries in the EIGRP topology table (including nonfeasible successor sources).

params.name: detail-links
params.description: (Optional) Displays all the topology entries with additional details.

params.name: pending
params.description: (Optional) Displays all the entries in the EIGRP topology table that are either waiting for an update from a neighbor or to reply to a neighbor. secondary-paths (Optional) Displays the secondary paths in the topology.

params.name: summary
params.description: (Optional) Displays a summary of the EIGRP topology table.

params.name: zero-successors
params.description: (Optional) Displays the available routes that have zero successors.

usageSummary: Use the show ip eigrp topology command to display topology entries, feasible and nonfeasible paths, metrics, and states.  This command can be used without any arguments or keywords to display only topology entries with feasible successors and feasible paths.  The all-links keyword displays all the paths, whether feasible or not, and the detail-links keyword displays additional details about these paths.

name: show ip eigrp traffic
detail: Display the number of Enhanced Interior Gateway Routing Protocol (EIGRP) packets sent and received.

params.name: vrf vrf-name
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the specified VRF.

params.name: vrf *
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about all VRFs.

params.name: autonomous-system-number
params.description: (Optional) Autonomous system number.

usageSummary: This command can be used to display information about EIGRP named configurations and EIGRP autonomous-system (AS) configurations.  This command displays the same information as the show eigrp address-family traffic command.  Cisco recommends using the show eigrp address-family traffic command.

name: show ip ospf
detail: Display general information about Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) routing processes.

params.name: process-id
params.description: (Optional) Process ID. If this argument is included, only information for the specified routing process is included.

name: show ip ospf border-routers
detail: Display the internal Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) routing table entries to an Area Border Router (ABR) and Autonomous System Boundary Router (ASBR).

name: show ip ospf database
detail: Display lists of information related to the Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) database for a specific router.
syntax: show ip ospf [process-id area-id] database show ip ospf [process-id area-id] database [adv-router [ip-address]] show ip ospf [process-id area-id] database [asbr-summary] [link-state-id] show ip ospf [process-id area-id] database [asbr-summary] [link-state-id] [adv-router [ip-address]] show ip ospf [process-id area-id] database [asbr-summary] [link-state-id] [self-originate] [link-state-id] show ip ospf [process-id area-id] database [database-summary] show ip ospf [process-id] database [external] [link-state-id] show ip ospf [process-id] database [external] [link-state-id] [adv-router [ip-address]] show ip ospf [process-id area-id] database [external] [link-state-id] [self-originate] [link-state-id] show ip ospf [process-id area-id] database [network] params.name: process-id
params.description: (Optional) Internal identification. It is locally assigned and can be any positive integer. The number used here is the number assigned administratively when enabling the OSPF routing process.

params.name: area-id
params.description: (Optional) Area number associated with the OSPF address range defined in the network router configuration command used to define the particular area.

params.name: adv-router [ip-address
params.description: (Optional) Displays all the LSAs of the specified router. If no IP address is included, the information is about the local router itself (in this case, the same as self-originate). Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17.15.x (Catalyst 9500 Switches) 1691 Routing show ip ospf database

params.name: link-state-id
params.description: (Optional) Portion of the Internet environment that is being described by the advertisement. The value entered depends on the advertisement’s LS type. It must be entered in the form of an IP address. When the link state advertisement is describing a network, the link-state-id can take one of two forms: The network’s IP address (as in type 3 summary link advertisements and in autonomous system external link advertisements). A derived address obtained from the link state ID. (Note that masking a network links advertisement’s link state ID with the network’s subnet mask yields the network’s IP address.) When the link state advertisement is describing a router, the link state ID is always the described router’s OSPF router ID. When an autonomous system external advertisement (LS Type = 5) is describing a default route, its link state ID is set to Default Destination (0.0.0.0).

params.name: asbr-summary
params.description: (Optional) Displays information only about the autonomous system boundary router summary LSAs.

params.name: database-summary
params.description: (Optional) Displays how many of each type of LSA for each area there are in the database, and the total.

params.name: external
params.description: (Optional) Displays information only about the external LSAs.

params.name: network
params.description: (Optional) Displays information only about the network LSAs.

params.name: nssa-external
params.description: (Optional) Displays information only about the NSSA external LSAs.

params.name: router
params.description: (Optional) Displays information only about the router LSAs.

params.name: self-originate
params.description: (Optional) Displays only self-originated LSAs (from the local router).

params.name: summary
params.description: (Optional) Displays information only about the summary LSAs.

params.name: lsa-summary
params.description: Displays the total LSA count per advertising router.

params.name: lsa-summary detail
params.description: Displays the count of each type of LSAs per advertising router.

params.name: lsa-summary router-id
params.description: Displays the count of each type of LSAs for the specified advertising router. router-id

usageSummary: The various forms of this command deliver information about different OSPF link state advertisements.  Command Reference, Cisco IOS XE 17. 15.

name: show ip ospf fast-reroute
detail: Display information for an OSPF per-prefix LFA FRR configuration.
syntax: show ip ospf [process-id] fast-reroute [prefix-summary | remote-lfa tunnels | ti-lfa [tunnels]]

params.name: process-id
params.description: (Optional) Internal identification. It is locally assigned and can be a positive integer. The number used here is the number assigned administratively when enabling the OSPF routing process.

params.name: prefix-summary
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the prefixes protected by the LFA FRR repair paths. remote-lfa tunnels (Optional) Displays information about the tunnel interfaces created by the remote LFA FRR.

params.name: ti-lfa [tunnels]
params.description: (Optional) Displays information about the topology-independent LFA.

usageSummary: Use the show ip ospf fast-reroute command to display information about the current tiebreaker policy.  Use the prefix-summary keyword to display the number of prefixes per area, per priority, and how many have repair paths, in absolute numbers and in percentages.  Use the remote-lfa tunnels keyword to display information about the tunnel interfaces created by the remote LFA FRR using the fast-reroute per-prefix remote-lfa tunnel command.

name: show ip ospf interface
detail: Display interface information related to Open Shortest Path First (OSPF).

params.name: process-id
params.description: (Optional) Process ID number. If this argument is included, only information for the specified routing process is included. The range is 1 to 65535.

params.name: type
params.description: (Optional) Interface type. If the type argument is included, only information for the specified interface type is included.

params.name: number
params.description: (Optional) Interface number. If the number argument is included, only information for the specified interface number is included.

params.name: brief
params.description: (Optional) Displays brief overview information for OSPF interfaces, states, addresses and masks, and areas on the device.

params.name: multicast
params.description: (Optional) Displays multicast information. topology topology-name (Optional) Displays OSPF-related information about the named topology instance.

params.name: topology base
params.description: (Optional) Displays OSPF-related information about the base topology.

name: show ip ospf neighbor
detail: Display Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) neighbor information on a per-interface basis.

params.name: interface-type interface-number
params.description: (Optional) Type and number associated with a specific OSPF interface.

params.name: neighbor-id
params.description: (Optional) Neighbor hostname or IP address in A.B.C.D format.

params.name: detail
params.description: (Optional) Displays all neighbors given in detail (lists all neighbors).

params.name: summary
params.description: (Optional) Displays total number summary of all neighbors.

params.name: per-instance
params.description: (Optional) Displays total number of neighbors in each neighbor state. The output is printed for each configured OSPF instance separately.

name: show ip ospf virtual-links
detail: Display parameters and the current state of Open Shortest Path First (OSPF) virtual links.
usageSummary: The information displayed by the showipospfvirtual-links command is useful in debugging OSPF routing operations.

name: summary-address
detail: Create aggregate addresses for Open Shortest Path First (OSPF).
syntax: summary-address commandsummary-address {ip-address mask | prefix mask} [not-advertise] [tag tag] [nssa-only]
noForm: no summary-address {ip-address mask | prefix mask} [not-advertise] [tag tag] [nssa-only]

params.name: ip-address
params.description: Summary address designated for a range of addresses.

params.name: mask
params.description: IP subnet mask used for the summary route.

params.name: prefix
params.description: IP route prefix for the destination.

params.name: not-advertise
params.description: (Optional) Suppresses routes that match the specified prefix/mask pair. This keyword applies to OSPF only.

params.name: tag
params.description: tag (Optional) Specifies the tag value that can be used as a “match” value for controlling redistribution via route maps. This keyword applies to OSPF only.

params.name: nssa-only
params.description: (Optional) Sets the nssa-only attribute for the summary route (if any) generated for the specified prefix, which limits the summary to not-so-stubby-area (NSSA) areas.

usageSummary: R outes learned from other routing protocols can be summarized.  The metric used to advertise the summary is the lowest metric of all the more specific routes.  This command helps reduce the size of the routing table.

name: timers throttle spf
detail: Turn on Open Shortest Path First ( OSPF) shortest path first (SPF) throttling.
syntax: timers throttle spf spf-start spf-hold spf-max-wait
noForm: no timers throttle spf spf-start spf-hold spf-max-wait

params.name: spf-start
params.description: Initial delay to schedule an SPF calculation after a change, in milliseconds. Range is from 1 to 600000. In OSPF for IPv6, the default value is 5000.

params.name: spf-hold
params.description: Minimum hold time between two consecutive SPF calculations, in milliseconds. Range is from 1 to 600000. In OSPF for IPv6, the default value is 10,000.

params.name: spf-max-wait
params.description: Maximum wait time between two consecutive SPF calculations, in milliseconds. Range is from 1 to 600000. In OSPF for IPv6, the default value is 10,000.

modes: Address family configuration (config-router-af) Router address family topology configuration (config-router-af-topology) Router configuration (config-router) OSPF for IPv6 router configuration (config-rtr)
usageSummary: The first wait interval between SPF calculations is the amount of time in milliseconds specified by the spf-startargument.  Each consecutive wait interval is two times the current hold level in milliseconds until the wait time reaches the maximum time in milliseconds as specified by the spf-max-wait argument.  Subsequent wait times remain at the maximum until the values are reset or a link-state advertisement (LSA) is received between SPF calculations.

name: topology
detail: Configure an EIGRP process to route IP traffic under the specified topology instance and to enter address-family topology configuration mode.

params.name: base
params.description: Specifies the base topology.

params.name: topology-name
params.description: Topology name. This value is case-sensitive.

params.name: tid number
params.description: Specifies the topology ID number. The range is from 1 to 65535.

usageSummary: Use this topology command in a Multitopology Routing (MTR) configuration to enable an EIGRP process under the specified topology.  Enter the topology command under address-family configuration mode.  Command configurations can be applied only to the topology instance.
